key_insight: k-means clustering on fingerprinted atomic configurations can select a much smaller, more informative training set than random sampling for fitting interatomic potentials.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster and more data-efficient calibration of classical molecular dynamics potentials, with fewer DFT-labeled configurations needed to achieve accurate energies and forces.
method: Build atomistic fingerprints from CrystalNN-based local structure descriptors and RDF features.
Cluster the full configuration pool with k-means to choose representative training configurations.
Validate selection quality by fitting an EAM titanium potential and comparing against random selection; visualize structure overlap with t-SNE.
evidence_signal: Using 1,800 titanium configurations, k-means consistently gave lower errors and lower variability than random selection, and about 30 selected configurations were reported as sufficient for a good EAM fit.
watch_item: The reported gains depend on the fingerprint design and may not transfer unchanged to different materials, defect types, or potential models without re-tuning.
paper_title: k-Means Clustering in Fingerprint-Based Configuration Selection for Fitting Interatomic Potentials
